business_context: DevForge is a Python + LangGraph stateful development orchestration kernel that manages multi-project software development work through role-based planning, executor dispatch, requirement patching, and seam governance — it is a development operating kernel, not a chat agent.

modules.description: Pydantic-style dataclass state models: WorkPackage, Project, Initiative, Seam, RequirementEvent, ExecutorPolicy, ExecutorResult, WorkspaceState
LangGraph meta-graph: builder, nodes, transitions, runtime_state, tool_enhanced_nodes
Executor adapter layer: base interface, ClaudeCode/Codex/Python adapters, subprocess transport, payload formatting, pull policy
Planning logic: concept decision, task shaping, gap analyzer, graph patch, retry policy/decision, design generator/validator, closure expander, acceptance
Workset selector: chooses which work packages to dispatch in a given cycle
Local knowledge base: domain/phase markdown content, knowledge registry, selectors, specializer, node knowledge packets
LLM provider abstraction: OpenRouter, Google, mock, httpx transport, config/factory, router
Four-layer persistence: SQLite/JSON snapshot store, JSONL event store, filesystem artifact store, JSON memory store
External tool integrations: Brave Search, FAL image generation, Stitch UI, XV validator
Context broker: pull policy resolution, context assembly for executor dispatch
Role system: role specs (product_manager, technical_architect, software_engineer, qa_engineer, etc.) and registry
Seam governance: seam verifier for cross-project contract verification
CLI entry point: fixture, snapshot, init, doctor, repl subcommands
Project config: llm_preferences, knowledge_preferences, pull_policy_overrides per project
Topology classifier: onboarding workspace candidates and modeling decisions

architecture_pattern: Layered orchestration kernel (state → planning → graph → executor)
